title: Fix owner principal in backend stable var

requirements.text: In backend/main.mo, update the stable owner principal variable to the exact text value 'q5rzs-s67ph-qtb5w-e66j5-2iqax-vlwa5-5pqxy-yosti-xhcis-ocfw6-yqe'. This must be set using Principal.fromText("q5rzs-s67ph-qtb5w-e66j5-2iqax-vlwa5-5pqxy-yosti-xhcis-ocfw6-yqe") in the stable var initializer. All hasPermission checks for the #owner role must compare the caller against this principal. Remove any previously hardcoded principal value. Do not rely on a roles TrieMap entry for owner — the owner check must be a direct equality comparison against this stable var so it cannot be overwritten.

quotes: Fix the owner principal please same issue.

acceptanceCriteria: The stable owner principal variable in main.mo is set to 'q5rzs-s67ph-qtb5w-e66j5-2iqax-vlwa5-5pqxy-yosti-xhcis-ocfw6-yqe' using Principal.fromText.
hasPermission(caller, #owner) returns true when and only when caller equals this principal.
No other principal value (old NNS principal or any other) appears as the owner in the codebase.
The owner principal check is a direct equality comparison against the stable var and cannot be overridden by the roles TrieMap.
Logging in with the app-derived Internet Identity principal grants full owner access including Settings, labor rate management, and owner-only metrics.

requirements.text: In backend/main.mo, ensure that all inventory endpoints (createPart, getPart, listParts, updatePart, deletePart, usePartOnJob) correctly accept calls from principals with at minimum the #authorized role, and also from the #owner principal. Verify the hasPermission guard on every inventory function uses the updated owner principal so the owner can perform all inventory CRUD operations.

quotes: Fix the owner principal please same issue.

acceptanceCriteria: The owner principal can call createPart, updatePart, deletePart, usePartOnJob, listParts, and getPart without receiving an access-denied error.
Authorized users (non-owner) can also call these endpoints successfully.
Unauthorized callers receive an access-denied error from all inventory endpoints.

constraints: The owner principal value must be exactly 'q5rzs-s67ph-qtb5w-e66j5-2iqax-vlwa5-5pqxy-yosti-xhcis-ocfw6-yqe' — do not alter or truncate it.
Do not change any frontend immutable paths.
All Motoko logic must remain in backend/main.mo as a single actor.
nonGoals: Do not change any frontend files for this fix.
Do not add new features or screens.
Do not modify the roles TrieMap logic beyond ensuring the owner stable var is used correctly for #owner checks.
